name: Baja California Sur (Mexico)

colors.symbolism: the field and details of the arms
the surrounding seas (the Pacific Ocean and the Gulf of California)
the sun, mineral wealth and aridity of the peninsula

description: A white field with the coat of arms of Baja California Sur centred.
history: Baja California Sur has no officially adopted state flag; the design shown — a white field bearing the state coat of arms — is in de facto use to represent the state.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the state coat of arms, in the typical pattern of Mexican state flags.

features.role: coat of arms of Baja California Sur
features.symbolism: The arms reference the peninsular geography of the state — the southern half of the Baja California peninsula — together with its sun, sea, missions and natural environment.
